subject: science
title: science_1.json
description: Grade 4 Science Assessment - Day 1: Plants, Ecosystems, and Matter

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of a plant's chlorophyll during photosynthesis?
questions.passage: Plants are autotrophs, meaning they create their own food. Through a process called photosynthesis, they capture light energy to transform water and carbon dioxide into glucose and oxygen.
options: To absorb water from the soil
To release oxygen into the air
To protect the plant from pests
questions.hint: Think about what gives leaves their green color and catches the sun's rays.
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the green pigment in plants that absorbs light energy from the sun, which is necessary for photosynthesis to occur.

questions.q: Which part of the plant is mainly responsible for anchoring it to the ground and absorbing water?
questions.explanation: Roots anchor the plant securely into the soil and draw up water and nutrients needed for growth.

questions.q: What gas do plants absorb from the air to perform photosynthesis?
questions.hint: It is the gas that humans and animals breathe out.
questions.explanation: Plants take in carbon dioxide from the atmosphere through tiny pores in their leaves to build sugars.

questions.q: If a plant's xylem tissue is damaged, what will the plant most likely struggle to transport?
options: Water from the roots to the leaves
Sugars from the leaves to the roots
Oxygen from the air to the stem
questions.answer: Water from the roots to the leaves
questions.hint: Think about the specialized tubes running upward from the underground systems.
questions.explanation: Xylem is the vascular tissue responsible for upward conduction of water and nutrients from the roots.

questions.q: Which animal group is characterized by having a backbone, being warm-blooded, and feeding its young with milk?
questions.hint: Humans, dogs, and whales belong to this specific group.
questions.explanation: Mammals are vertebrates that have fur or hair, are warm-blooded, and nurse their young with milk.

questions.q: How many legs does a true insect have?
questions.hint: Count the legs of an ant or a beetle.
questions.explanation: All adult insects belong to the class Insecta and possess exactly six jointed legs.

questions.q: An organism that breaks down dead plants and animals into simpler nutrients is known as a:
questions.hint: Mushrooms, fungi, and certain bacteria do this recycling work.
questions.explanation: Decomposers break down organic matter from dead organisms, returning valuable nutrients back into the soil.

questions.q: Why do desert animals like the fennec fox have exceptionally large ears?
options: To help radiate body heat and stay cool
To store excess water for dry periods
To look intimidating to larger predators
To blend in with the sandy environment
questions.answer: To help radiate body heat and stay cool
questions.hint: Think about how heat moves away from a wide surface area.
questions.explanation: Large ears have a high surface area filled with blood vessels, allowing the animal to release excess body heat in hot desert climates.

questions.q: Which state of matter has a definite volume but takes the shape of its container?
questions.hint: Think about water when you pour it from a pitcher into a glass.
questions.explanation: Liquids have a fixed volume, but their particles can move past each other, allowing them to flow and change shape based on the container.

questions.q: At what temperature on the Celsius scale does pure liquid water freeze into ice?
questions.hint: It is the baseline value for the freezing point of water.
questions.explanation: Water changes its state from liquid to solid ice at its freezing point of 0 degrees Celsius.
